title: Diagnosing Task Insensitivity in Language Agents
abstract: arXiv:2606.26918v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models can serve as capable long-horizon agents, but their out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization remains weak. We identify a key source of this failure as task insensitivity: when faced with similar but distinct tasks, models might apply patterns learned during training and fail to solve the task at hand. We show that models often continue with actions aligned with the original task even when the instruction is semantically corrupted and cannot be directly answered. We further find that, when we replace the task description in a trained prompt with another similar but distinct task, the model may still output the same action. This behavior is accompanied by a consistent training-time attention drift away from task tokens and toward local observations, suggesting an optimization bias toward shortcuts. To mitigate this problem, we propose Task-Perturbed NLL Optimization, a lightweight contrastive regularizer that explicit

translated_title: 诊断语言代理中的任务不敏感性
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26918v1 公告类型：新 摘要：大型语言模型能够作为强大的长程代理，但其分布外泛化能力仍然较弱。我们识别出这一失败的一个关键来源是任务不敏感性：当面对相似但不同的任务时，模型可能会应用训练中学到的模式，从而无法解决当前任务。我们表明，即使指令在语义上被破坏且无法直接回答，模型也常常继续执行与原始任务一致的动作。我们进一步发现，当我们将训练提示中的任务描述替换为另一个相似但不同的任务时，模型可能仍会输出相同的动作。这种行为伴随着训练期间注意力从任务标记向局部观测的一致漂移，表明存在一种偏向于捷径的优化偏差。为缓解这一问题，我们提出了任务扰动负对数似然优化，一种轻量级的对比正则化方法，显式地……
tldr: 本文揭示了语言模型在长程任务中因过度依赖训练模式而忽略任务差异的“任务不敏感性”问题，并提出了一种轻量级对比正则化方法来缓解该问题。
en_tdlr: This paper identifies the "task insensitivity" problem in language agents, where models over-rely on training patterns and ignore task differences, and proposes a lightweight contrastive regularizer to mitigate it.
